description: Atmosphere system test vectors — verifies star count, day/night cycle solar altitude, fog parameters, cloud count, moon and planet visual radii. Location defaults to Auckland NZ (-36.85, 174.76).

vectors.description: Star count: the catalog contains exactly 2865 real stars (magnitude <= 5.5) from HYG/Yale Bright Star Catalog.

expected.note: 6 bytes per star: RA (uint16), Dec (uint16), magnitude (uint8), B-V colour index (uint8).

vectors.description: Solar altitude at noon Auckland summer solstice (Dec 21). Sun should be high in the sky (near zenith for southern hemisphere summer).

expected.note: At Auckland (36.85S) on summer solstice with declination -23.44, solar altitude at noon is approximately 90 - |lat - dec| = 90 - |-36.85 - (-23.44)| = 90 - 13.41 = 76.6 degrees. sin(76.6) ≈ 0.97.

vectors.description: Solar altitude at midnight Auckland winter solstice (Jun 21). Sun should be deeply below the horizon.

expected.note: At midnight in winter, the sun is roughly 76+ degrees below the horizon. sin(elevation) is deeply negative (<< -0.35), placing the scene firmly in Deep Night palette.

vectors.description: Fog density range: base FOG_NEAR=50, FOG_FAR=130. Weather modifies via fogMultiplier.

expected.note: fogMultiplier from lerp3(wi, 1.0, 0.65, 0.6). Applied to fog distances. During storms above snowline, fog collapses to 30/80m for blizzard whiteout.

vectors.description: Cloud count: CONFIG.CLOUD_COUNT = 20 cloud groups. Each group contains multiple puffs (2-10 depending on archetype).

expected.note: 20 groups with weighted random archetype selection. Total puffs approximately 80-120 across all groups.

vectors.description: Moon visual radius: CONFIG.MOON_VISUAL_RADIUS = 1.75. CircleGeometry with 32 segments at distance 135m from player.

expected.phaseShader: Sharp alpha cutoff: lit side opaque, shadow side transparent
expected.note: Moon is rendered as a CircleGeometry disc with custom ShaderMaterial. Phase computed from synodic period (29.53059 days).

vectors.description: Planet visual radius: CONFIG.PLANET_VISUAL_RADIUS = 0.6. Base size scaled by magnitude for each of 5 naked-eye planets.

expected.note: Venus at mag -4.4 appears at scale ~2.1x base. Jupiter and Saturn even larger due to high absolute magnitude.

vectors.description: Day/night palette transitions: 6 elevation bands drive palette blending.

expected.note: Deep Night is distinctly darker than Night. Midnight should use Deep Night (sin elevation << -0.35). Early evening around -0.2 is Night/Deep Night blend.

vectors.description: Star rendering parameters: size and brightness derived from magnitude.

expected.note: Sirius (mag -1.46) gets size ~4.5 and brightness ~1.3. Faintest stars (mag 5.5) get size ~1.0 and brightness 0.35.
